insight: Haiti's performance during hydration breaks in the 2026 World Cup has been notable, as they managed to gain momentum in both of their breaks, with a mean increase of 0.500 points per minute. This suggests that the team is able to use the breaks to regroup and refocus, coming back onto the field with renewed energy and intensity. The fact that they were able to maintain this momentum when not pressing before the break, with an average increase of 0.500 points per minute in these situations, indicates that Haiti is able to capitalize on the brief pause in play to adjust their strategy and make a strong push forward. Overall, the hydration breaks seem to have a positive impact on Haiti's gameplay, allowing them to shift the momentum in their favor and potentially change the course of the match.
